string 2: This elevator isn't moving.
Maybe there's another elevator somewhere?

Are you using the stairs to get to the roof? Why not try
the elevator?

It was recently discovered that Digimon had characteristics
of both organic matter and electric signals.

Your Digivice uses the electronic aspects of Digimon to
summon them into action.

The Digivice can also convert the Digimon equivalent of DNA
into binary code, which can then be replicated like data.

We call this system of creating new Digimon "{fc9Converting}."
Try using this feature to augment your forces.
Phew... It looks like we made it somehow.

I don't want to witness all this fighting. Let's see how high the 
elevator can take us.
What happened here...?
Could this be what the Wall was built to hide...?

Maybe something's happening on the roof of this building
like that girl said...

We can't ignore this. Cast aside common sense here.
Anomalies always take a turn for the unexpected.

Target coordinates set to "Tokyo Metropolitan Government 
Building rooftop." Your first priority is to find a way up.

Right now, you should head to the roof of the government
building.
You know, I have a feeling that elevator works...
Maybe we can get there from the other side? Let's find a way!

I have a report for you, Agent. The latest update patch is
available for your Digivice.

You now have access to Agent Skills. They should be a great
help in executing your mission. Go on. Give them a try.

We have to go to the south wing to get to the roof.
I wonder if we can get there from the other side...
Your Digivice also has an {fc9Analyze} {l2} function.

It instantly scans your surroundings and marks anything
suspicious. The Digimon in your Digivice help with this.

Their sense of sight and smell helps pinpoint places of concern.
A useful feature when you're unsure of where to go.
There's a wall here that could collapse at any moment.
Thank you for the help! And good luck!
It doesn't look like we can get back this way.
It looks like it could be destroyed with a DigiAttack {r2}.

Let me tell you about {fc9Digivolution}. The person who
came up with the basic theories was your adoptive father...

...Dr. Yuki, the founder of ADAMAS. He referred to phase
electron lifeforms as "semi-cyber beings."

Dr. Yuki's research was ignored in academic circles, but was
carried on by like-minded individuals in the organization.

And that research led to the discovery of Digivolution. As Digimon
gain experience in battle, they go through changes.

You might even have some Digimon that are ready to
Digivolve now. Check your Digivice and see.

There's no response... Biyomon mentioned something
about a war between Digimon...
There's no response... Is there truly a war between Digimon...?
They've collapsed... Was this the doing of another Digimon?

Let me tell you about Digimon {fc9Digivolution}. The person who
came up with the basic theories was your adoptive father...

...Dr. Yuki, the founder of ADAMAS. He referred to phase
electron lifeforms as "semi-cyber beings."

Dr. Yuki's research was ignored in academic circles, but was
carried on by like-minded individuals in the organization.

And that research led to the discovery of Digivolution. As Digimon
gain experience in battle, they undergo transformations.

You might even have some Digimon that are ready to Digivolve
now. Check your Digivice and see.
Boss Kuwagamon appears.
Battle with Boss Kuwagamon
Hold on! An anomaly has been detected ahead!

Be sure to restore your Digimon and be ready for whatever
happens next!

I can't predict what awaits you ahead. Prepare for the
worst, and don't forget about {fc9Converting} and {fc9Digivolution}.

These functions are based on the theories given to us by
Dr. Yuki. His inventions contributed greatly to society.

Yet so few know of him because he rarely made public
apperances and he gave up all the rights to his work...

He poured his own private funds into the establishment of
ADAMAS to protect humanity from the threat of anomalies.

No one is more suited to carry on his legacy than you, who bears
his name. Master {fc9Converting} and {fc9Digivolution} well!
